title: Malagarr battle droid
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:Sith_battle_droid_web.jpg]] |name=Malagarr battle droid |creator= |manufacturer= |designer= |homeworld=[[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] |line= |model= |class=[[Battle droid/Legends|Battle droid]] |degree= |cost= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender= |sensor= |plating= |armament=3 [[Vibro-ax/Legends|Vibro-axs]] |equipment= |affiliation=[[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|I thought Q-7N was the only droid who guarded this fortress!"
"K-Klik! You thought wrong!|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and the Sith battle droid|''[[Star Wars Missions 1: Assault on Yavin Four]]''}} '''Malagarr battle droids''' were used as [[Security droid/Legends|security droids]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to protect [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] treasure in the numerous [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] temples. They had three arms, with each wielding a [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-ax]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] a [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel soldier]] chanced upon several of them, and later, [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] destroyed one. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars Missions 1: Assault on Yavin Four]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars Missions 17: Darth Vader's Return]]'' *{{StoryCite|series=Star Wars Kids (1997)|srtext=''Star Wars Kids'' (1997)|issue1=1|issue2=5|story=The Rebel Thief}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Battle droid models]]
